summary:
• Escalating geopolitical tensions in the Hormuz Strait and broader Iran conflict pose the most acute near-term supply risk to Western Hemisphere markets, with the IEA warning of the largest-ever energy security threat and crude prices already reacting to heightened regional instability.

• Middle East supply disruption risks are being amplified by the Russia-Iran nexus and potential Iran war scenarios, though Goldman Sachs indicates rapid output recovery potential if tensions de-escalate, creating significant price volatility for downstream refiners globally.

• Western Hemisphere domestic supply appears stable with GeoPark showing continued production growth and new capital flowing into oil and gas projects, but geopolitical shocks affecting global crude markets remain the dominant supply-chain risk for regional energy security.
